agent_reply: - Initializes Serial, `AudioManager`, touch buttons, speaker mode, and recording.
- Starts a core 0 task for UI/control:
- Stops I2S, drains/stops the recording, prints the final path/byte count, then idles.
- Prints once-per-second status with mode, mic peak, file bytes, and recorder write timing stats.
Verified with `pio run`: build succeeded. One note: Git currently sees `src/tests/test_micrec.cpp` as untracked, even though it existed in the workspace.

test_micrec: stop requested: right touch button
the second time the mic is enabled, no data arrived, we need to fix this
agent_reply: Yep, the failure mode is the unused `mic2bt` FIFO filling up.

In `test_micrec`, there is no Bluetooth/HFP consumer draining `micToBluetoothFifo()`, so after the first mic section fills that FIFO, later mic sections never read from I2S and never queue to `mic2file`.

I fixed it locally in [test_micrec.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_micrec.cpp) by draining/discarding `AudioManager::micToBluetoothFifo()` before each `pump_mic2bt()` call in mic mode. That keeps the BT-side FIFO from blocking the file recording path during this test.

user_prompt: in `lib\AudioFifo\AudioFifo.h`, add something similar to `muted` but call it `choked`, it simply won't let queue happen, but return success.
there are operating modes that don't use Bluetooth, like a local memo recording, and I want this as a mechanism to not need having to drain the BT FIFO
when this is implemented, let the `test_micrec` simply set the choke flag instead of draining the FIFO
agent_reply: In [AudioFifo.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:74), `choked` now behaves like a sink:
- `availableToWrite()` reports full capacity while choked, so producers like `pump_mic2bt()` keep running.
In [test_micrec.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_micrec.cpp:165), the test now chokes `micToBluetoothFifo()` instead of draining it, and unchokes it on stop/failure.
Verification: `pio run` passed successfully. There is still the existing packed-member warning in `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, unrelated to this change.

user_prompt: I marked two locations in test_micrec.cpp where I want time measurements, do them using micros but report them back in floating milliseconds
agent_reply: Both now print as floating milliseconds, e.g. `AudioManager init took 123.456 ms`.

user_prompt: move literally all insstances of idle_forever into `lib\UtilFuncs\utilfuncs.cpp`, it doesn't need to be fancy
agent_reply: Done. `idle_forever()` now lives in [utilfuncs.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.cpp) with its declaration in [utilfuncs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.h), and the local copies were removed from all the test files.
It’s intentionally plain now: just loops forever with `delay(1000)`.

commit_message: clean up some tests a bit

user_prompt: I added using the screen as a status indicator inside `src\tests\test_micrec.cpp`, fix any mistakes
agent_reply: - `display` was local to `test_micrec()` but used inside `micrec_core0_task()`.
- Adding a local `idle_forever()` conflicted with the existing global one from `utilfuncs.h`.
I added a small `set_status_color()` helper using `M5.Display` directly, so the core-0 task can safely set:

user_prompt: fix any mistakes related to this invokation highlighted in the IDE
agent_reply: The issue was that `Clock.syncToCompileTime()` was called before `M5.begin(cfg)`, so the M5 RTC object may not have been initialized yet. I also added the missing `ClockAgent.h` include.
The issue was that `Clock.syncToCompileTime()` was called before `M5.begin(cfg)`, so the M5 RTC object may not have been initialized yet. I also added the missing `ClockAgent.h` include.

commit_message: visual indicator for mic recorder test

user_prompt: make test_micrec also report micScaledPeakLevel so I can see it working, make the report every 500 ms too
agent_reply: The status interval is now `500 ms`, and the log line reports both `mic_peak` and `mic_scaled_peak`.
Verified with `pio run`: build succeeds. As usual, the build regenerated the `src/version.c` nonce.

commit_message: added a mic preprocessor, HPF and AGC

user_prompt: avoid having `M5GFX& display` as a parameter for functions and methods, there should be a global reference to the display for anything unrelated to FlyGui, and if FlyGui is involved, there should be a way to dig through trees to get a reference to it, or just use the global reference
agent_reply: - Updated direct display users to use `thefly_display` instead of threading a local `display` reference or calling `M5.Display` directly.
- Kept `FlyGui::display()` as a convenience getter that returns the global display, so tree-based GUI code can still ask the GUI for its screen if needed.
